name: constitution.article_1.rights
title: Rights of the Citizens
description: Fundamental rights that constrain all other laws. Any law violating these meta-rules is void on passage. These tensions run at maximum priority and act as constitutional guards — they invalidate laws, not enforce them directly.

tensions.name: guard_free_speech
tensions.description: Section 1: No law shall abridge the freedom of speech. Any law with censorship provisions is void.

emit.value: Violates Article I, Section 1: Freedom of Speech

tensions.name: guard_property_rights
tensions.description: Section 2: Every citizen has the right to own and hold property. No law may authorize seizure without due process and just compensation.

emit.value: Violates Article I, Section 2: Right to Property — seizure without due process

tensions.name: guard_right_to_vote
tensions.description: Section 3: Every citizen of legal standing has the right to vote in all elections. No law may disenfranchise citizens except by conviction of treason.

emit.value: Violates Article I, Section 3: Right to Vote

tensions.name: guard_fair_trial
tensions.description: Section 4: Every citizen accused of a crime has the right to a fair trial by jury. No law may authorize punishment without trial.

emit.value: Violates Article I, Section 4: Right to Fair Trial
